name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union at 2829 N Lombard St, Portland, OR 97217 in the North Portland neighborhood is open 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM daily. Call +1 503-737-0317.
description_long:
Western Union is a well-established financial services company that operates one of the largest networks for international and domestic money transfers. The company has built its business around providing accessible financial services through a distributed network of agent locations rather than traditional brick-and-mortar branches. This agent-based model allows Western Union to reach consumers in diverse locations including grocery stores, pharmacies, and convenience retailers across the United States.

Western Union's service offerings center on money movement and payment capabilities. Customers can send money online or in-store, pick up cash transfers at agent locations, track transfers in real-time, pay bills, purchase money orders, and reload prepaid cards. The company offers both online transaction initiation with in-store payment options and fully digital transactions. Additionally, Western Union provides mobile top-up services, inmate payment services, and the ability to receive money on a card. The platform operates 24/7 for online transactions, though in-store payment availability depends on individual agent location hours.

What distinguishes Western Union is its extensive physical distribution network—the website shows over 150 agent locations accessible in search results alone—combined with digital accessibility. Unlike traditional banks or credit unions, Western Union doesn't require account opening or membership. The company has built brand recognition specifically for international remittances and serves populations relying on non-traditional banking channels. Their multi-language support (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) reflects their target demographic.

However, Western Union's service comes with important caveats. The website provides limited transparent pricing information—users must estimate prices through their tool rather than seeing rates upfront. No mention appears of fee structures, exchange rates for international transfers, or comparative pricing. The company operates in the check-cashing and money services category, which typically involves higher fees than traditional banking alternatives. Customers should verify specific costs before initiating transfers, and this service is best suited for those without traditional bank accounts or those specifically needing to send money internationally rather than as a primary financial institution.
pros: 24/7 online transaction capability for money transfers and payments
Extensive agent location network including major retailers (Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Kroger)
Multi-language support in six languages to serve diverse populations
Multiple service options: send money, pick up cash, pay bills, purchase money orders, mobile top-up
Real-time transfer tracking and mobile payment capabilities
No account requirement—walk-in service available at agent locations
Ability to receive money directly on a prepaid card
cons: No transparent fee structure or exchange rate information displayed on website
Service availability limited by agent location hours despite 24/7 online initiation
Typically higher fees compared to traditional banks and digital-only money transfer competitors
Limited product information—website focuses on location finding rather than service details and pricing
Not a substitute for banking services; no checking, savings, or credit-building products
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers without traditional bank accounts
International remittance senders supporting family overseas
Consumers needing immediate cash pickup rather than account deposits
Non-English speakers in immigrant communities with multilingual support needs
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers without traditional bank accounts who need to send or receive money through a physical location, or those specifically sending international remittances. The primary caveat is that Western Union charges significantly higher fees than banks and modern digital money transfer services—users should always estimate costs before using their service, as fees can substantially exceed alternatives like ACH transfers or online remittance platforms.
services: In-store money transfer pickup with cash
